content.plain_english: This is a recap week. Your child is going back over the year's main learning — counting to 100, simple addition and subtraction, halves and quarters, shapes — and applying it to mixed problems.
content.in_school: Lessons are gentler. Teachers bring out old favourites: cubes, ten-frames, number lines, shape sets. Children pick up topics from earlier in the year and revisit any they found tricky. There is no testing.
content.home_activity: Look through any maths book or sheet your child has brought home this term. Pick one page and ask them to walk you through it. End by asking: what is one thing you can do in maths now that you couldn't last September? Five minutes.
dinner_table_questions: What is one maths thing you found tricky in September that feels easy now?
If you had to teach one maths trick to a Reception child, what would it be?
Show me three ways to make 10.

key_vocabulary.definition: Look back over what you have learned.
Use words to describe how you worked something out.
Look at two things to see how they differ.
Something that repeats in a regular order.

example_questions: Show 35 as a number of tens and ones.
content.times_table_tip: Year 1 children practise counting in 2s, 5s and 10s. Pick the count you find trickiest — say 5s — and chant it once a day for a week.
